text.content: French macarons are notoriously one of the hardest desserts to make, but there are only 5 ingredients! Here are some tips to make sure they turn out right every time!

rich_text.plain_text: French macarons are notoriously one of the hardest desserts to make, but there are only 5 ingredients! Here are some tips to make sure they turn out right every time!

text.content: 1. Make sure your measurements are precise! If you pack your ingredients into measuring cups, your ratios may be off.

rich_text.plain_text: 1. Make sure your measurements are precise! If you pack your ingredients into measuring cups, your ratios may be off.

text.content: 2. Ensure the meringue is whipped to stiff peaks. Over or under-mixing will negatively affect the texture.

rich_text.plain_text: 2. Ensure the meringue is whipped to stiff peaks. Over or under-mixing will negatively affect the texture.

text.content: 3. When folding the macaron batter, the texture should fall off the spatula like a ribbon. Over-mixing will result in flat cookies and under-mixing makes the top of the cookies burst.

rich_text.plain_text: 3. When folding the macaron batter, the texture should fall off the spatula like a ribbon. Over-mixing will result in flat cookies and under-mixing makes the top of the cookies burst.

text.content: 4. Allow the cookies to dry on the sheet for 30 minutes before baking to ensure the feet form!

rich_text.plain_text: 4. Allow the cookies to dry on the sheet for 30 minutes before baking to ensure the feet form!

text.content: 2 cups of powdered sugar. 1 cup of almond flour. Sift - whisk together (set it aside). Separate 3 eggs and add just the 3 egg whites to the bowl mixture thing. Pipe the macarons on parchment paper. Bang tray on the table or bench to get out air bubbles. Bake at 300degrees for 15-20mins. The sign of a true macaron should have a ring around the edge called "feet". Fill the macarons with what ever you want. He made a dolce delece??(something) pastry cream.

link_mention.title: No-Knead Salt Bread
link_mention.description: Salt bread, also known as shiopan in Japan and sogumbbang in Korea, is a soft, buttery roll with a lightly crisp exterior and a butter center. It’s simple in ingredients but incredibly rich in flavor, with a fluffy, milky crumb and just enough salt on top to balance the sweetness.

link_mention.title: Salt Bread (Shiopan)
link_mention.description: Salt bread, or shio pan, is a beloved Japanese-style bread roll that has recently taken Asia by storm, especially in Korea, where you’ll now find it in every bakery. What makes salt bread unique is its simple yet magical combination of soft, fluffy dough rolled around a stick of butter. As the bread bakes, the butter melts and seeps into the dough, creating a glossy, golden crust while frying the bottom of the roll to a crisp, buttery perfection. The contrast of the tender inside and the shatteringly crisp, salty base is what makes salt bread so irresistible.
